methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $8.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.1B - $2.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Sierra Space, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company, carries a current post-money valuation of $8.0B. The valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Impulse Space.

Two methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $8.0B last post-money valuation, implying $13.6B at the next funding stage. Peer-median analysis anchors to the $1.5B median across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (range: $1.1B–$2.3B). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures. The median implied valuation across applicable methods is $7.5B, with a triangulated range of $1.3B (low) to $15.6B (high). The wide spread reflects material tension between stage-progression methodology, which assumes continued capital market appetite, and peer-median methodology, which suggests substantial valuation compression relative to the current post-money. Key uncertainties include Sierra Space's revenue trajectory, capital intensity of near-term milestones, and broader sector funding conditions.
